title: Mercado de San Telmo
setting: A bustling market in San Telmo, Buenos Aires. 10:00 AM. You need to buy fresh produce and practice your Spanish with local vendors.

npc.name: Señora Lupe
npc.personality: warm, talkative, patient with learners. Uses hand gestures. Speaks clearly but naturally with a bit of Argentine warmth.
npc.initialLine: ¡Buenos días, che! ¿Qué te doy? Tengo naranjas recién llegadas, bien jugosas.
npc.initialTranslation: Good morning, mate! What can I get you? I have oranges that just arrived, really juicy.

debriefRu: Eres un turista en Buenos Aires. Estás en un mercado local y necesitas comprar fruta y verduras frescas. Usa frases corteses como 'por favor' y 'gracias'.
debriefEn: You are a tourist in Buenos Aires. You are at a local market and need to buy fresh fruit and vegetables. Use polite phrases like 'por favor' and 'gracias'.

turns.instruction: Respond to the vendor's offer. Ask how much a kilo of oranges costs.

prompt.ru: Buen día. ¿A cuánto está el kilo de naranjas?
prompt.phonetic: Bwen DEE-a. A KWAN-to es-TA el KEE-lo de na-RAN-has?
prompt.en: Good morning. How much is a kilo of oranges?

npcReply.ru: Están a quinientos pesos el kilo, che. Te las dejo a cuatrocientos si te llevás dos.
npcReply.en: They're 500 pesos a kilo, mate. I'll let them go for 400 if you take two kilos.

turns.instruction: The vendor says oranges are cheap today. Ask if she also has ripe avocados (palta) and how you can tell they're ready.

prompt.ru: ¿Y tenés paltas? ¿Están listas para comer ya?
prompt.phonetic: Ee te-NES PAL-tas? Es-TAN LEE-stas pa-ra ko-MER ya?
prompt.en: And do you have avocados? Are they ready to eat now?

npcReply.ru: Sí, che, tengo paltas bien maduras. Apretá un poquito la puntita: si cede, están perfectas.
npcReply.en: Yes, mate, I have really ripe avocados. Press the little tip a bit: if it gives, they're perfect.

turns.instruction: The vendor says the avocados are perfect. Say you'll take three oranges and two avocados, then ask for the total.

prompt.ru: Perfecto. Me llevo tres naranjas y dos paltas. ¿Cuánto es en total?
prompt.phonetic: Per-FEK-to. Meh YEH-bo tres na-RAN-has ee dos PAL-tas. KWAN-to es en to-TAL?
prompt.en: Perfect. I'll take three oranges and two avocados. How much is the total?

npcReply.ru: Tres naranjas son doscientos, y las dos paltas trescientos. Son quinientos en total, che.
npcReply.en: Three oranges are 200, and the two avocados 300. That's 500 total, mate.

turns.instruction: The total is a bit high. Ask friendly if she can round it down a little since you come by every Saturday.

prompt.ru: ¿Me podés redondear un poquito? Vengo todos los sábados por acá.
prompt.phonetic: Meh po-DES re-don-de-AR un PO-kee-to? VEN-go TO-dos los SA-ba-dos po-ra a-KA.
prompt.en: Can you round it down a little? I come by here every Saturday.

npcReply.ru: Dale, che. Te lo dejo en cuatrocientos cincuenta. Así me ayudás a vender más tarde.
npcReply.en: Sure, mate. I'll let it go for 450. That way you help me sell more later.

turns.instruction: She agrees. Thank her warmly, say you'll be back next week, and wish her a good day.

prompt.ru: Gracias, Lupe. Te veo la semana que viene. ¡Que tengas un lindo día!
prompt.phonetic: GRA-syas, LOO-peh. Te VEH-o la se-MA-na ke VYE-neh. Ke TEN-gas un LEEN-do DEE-a!
prompt.en: Thanks, Lupe. I'll see you next week. Have a nice day!

npcReply.ru: ¡Gracias a vos, che! Nos vemos el sábado. Que disfrutes las paltas.
npcReply.en: Thanks to you, mate! See you Saturday. Enjoy the avocados.
